Contract §6 (WEDGE_EVENT_CONTRACT_AND_GOVERNANCE_PHASE1.md): 'Can do now: Finalise this contract, implement first-party endpoint design in WEDGE-METRICS-B.' — endpoint design is explicitly permitted. 'Must review before prod instrumentation: Legal/privacy on notice + consent.' Prompt Stage 1C item 4 requires explicit verification and recording of privacy prerequisites. Prompt scope item 4 (collection posture) requires matching §5. These prerequisites are a documented gate, not a blocker to implementation structure. Prompt correctly defers vendor selection behind the DPA/notice/consent gates.

Scope items 1-7 (lines 115-278) define exact required deliverables with explicit requirements. OUT OF SCOPE (lines 305-316) prohibits: BI/warehouse, experimentation, payment/billing, fabricated conversion/segment/carry-through, backend reasoning changes, broad logging, launch-posture reopening. Scope item 3 (payload minimisation) explicitly prohibits raw biomarkers, questionnaire answers, health content, tokens in event payloads.

Scope item 7 (lines 259-278): frontend tests for event emission utilities; integration tests for key flows; minimal backend tests if first-party endpoint added; no broad analytics test suite. Validation targets: agreed events fire from intended surfaces; deferred events not misrepresented as live; JSON export not labelled as clinician report download; payload minimisation respected; no backend analytical behaviour changed. Bounded and proportionate to scope.

Five explicit STOP conditions (lines 359-373): (1) implementation requires deviating from event contract without amendment; (2) collection posture cannot be implemented without unresolved privacy/vendor risk; (3) only way to show progress is to fake blocked metrics; (4) work drifts into warehouse/BI/experimentation; (5) work alters product semantics to produce analytics events. Condition 3 directly protects against paid_conversion/segment/carry-through fabrication. Condition 1 protects event naming and semantic integrity.

Implementation constraints (lines 322-340): 'Product behaviour should not change beyond instrumentation — Do not alter analytical logic, truth hierarchy, or clinician-report semantics.' Intelligence Core untouched: authStore, upload.ts, analysisStore, results page are product surfaces, not analytical reasoning components. analysisStore.startAnalysis() calls AnalysisService.startAnalysis() (API call) — instrumentation adds side-effect analytics calls only, not changes to the API call or its response handling. Emitted analytical reasoning identical to pre-sprint state.

OUT OF SCOPE explicitly excludes: BI/warehouse, experimentation, payment/billing implementation, fabricated paid-conversion/segment/carry-through events, backend reasoning changes, broad logging, launch-posture re-decision. Stage 1C §6 lists 6 explicit exclusions. STOP conditions 3 and 4 halt any drift. The optional backend endpoint (scope item 8) is bounded: 'minimal schema, no broad telemetry platform, no drift into operational warehouse work.'
